title: pimelea oreophila subsp. hetera
intro: Pimelea oreophila subsp. hetera is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant that is native to Tasmania, Australia. It belongs to the Thymelaeaceae family and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant is known to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
